ucs.title: [LLM] APT10 (Stone Panda / menuPass) DLL side-loading via signed Oracle/McAfee/VMware host binaries
ucs.description: Hunts APT10's signature initial-execution chain: drop a legitimately-signed third-party binary (jjs.exe, GUP.exe, mcoemcpy.exe, EhStorAuthn.exe, vmtoolsd.exe, etc.) into a non-install path together with a malicious sibling DLL (jli.dll, libcurl.dll, McUtil.dll, vmtools.dll) so the signed host loads APT10 implants like ChChes, RedLeaves, ANEL, UPPERCUT and PlugX. The host-binary + DLL pairings, together with the run-from-Temp/Public/ProgramData footprint, are documented across PwC, FireEye, JPCERT and Trend Micro reporting on Cloudhopper / Operation ChessMaster.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("jjs.exe","GUP.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","EhStorAuthn.exe","rasphone.exe","chrome_frame_helper.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","mscoree.exe","sllauncher.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval suspect_path=if(match(process_path,"(?i)\\\\(Users|Public|ProgramData|AppData|Temp|Windows\\\\Temp|Intel|PerfLogs)\\\\") AND NOT match(process_path,"(?i)\\\\Program Files( \\(x86\\))?\\\\"),1,0)
| where suspect_path=1
| join type=inner host process_id [
    | tstats summariesonly=false count values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as dll_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("jli.dll","libcurl.dll","McUtil.dll","vmtools.dll","EhStorShell.dll","chrome_frame_helper.dll","mscoree.dll","VERSION.dll") by host Filesystem.process_id Filesystem.file_name
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
    | rename process_id as process_id
  ]
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| table firstTime lastTime host user process_name process_path cmdline parent file_name dll_path dll_hash
ucs.kql:
// APT10 DLL side-loading: signed host binary in a non-install path loads the matching APT10 sideload DLL adjacent to it
let SideloadHosts = dynamic(["jjs.exe","gup.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","ehstorauthn.exe","rasphone.exe","chrome_frame_helper.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","mscoree.exe","sllauncher.exe"]);
let SideloadDlls   = dynamic(["jli.dll","libcurl.dll","mcutil.dll","vmtools.dll","ehstorshell.dll","chrome_frame_helper.dll","version.dll"]);
let KnownGoodPaths = dynamic([@"c:\program files\", @"c:\program files (x86)\", @"c:\windows\system32\", @"c:\windows\syswow64\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (SideloadHosts)
| where FileName in~ (SideloadDlls)
// adjacent-DLL is the actual sideload pattern — DLL written next to the loader
| extend HostDir = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath), DllDir = tolower(FolderPath)
| where HostDir == DllDir
// strip legitimate vendor install paths
| where not(HostDir startswith_cs @"c:\program files\")
     and not(HostDir startswith_cs @"c:\program files (x86)\")
     and not(HostDir startswith_cs @"c:\windows\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          User = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          HostBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          HostBinPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          HostBinSha256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          HostBinCmdLine = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          LoadedDll = FileName,
          LoadedDllPath = FolderPath,
          LoadedDllSha256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT10 DLL side-loading via Sysmon EID 7 (ImageLoad). Requires Sysmon → WindowsEvent
let SideloadHosts = dynamic(["jjs.exe","gup.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","ehstorauthn.exe","rasphone.exe","chrome_frame_helper.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","mscoree.exe","sllauncher.exe"]);
let SideloadDlls   = dynamic(["jli.dll","libcurl.dll","mcutil.dll","vmtools.dll","ehstorshell.dll","chrome_frame_helper.dll","version.dll"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend Image       = tolower(tostring(EventData.Image)),
         ImageLoaded = tolower(tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded)),
         User        = tostring(EventData.User),
         SignerName  = tostring(EventData.SignatureStatus)
| extend HostBin  = extract(@"\\([^\\]+)$", 1, Image),
         LoadedDll = extract(@"\\([^\\]+)$", 1, ImageLoaded),
         HostDir   = extract(@"^(.+)\\[^\\]+$", 1, Image),
         DllDir    = extract(@"^(.+)\\[^\\]+$", 1, ImageLoaded)
| where HostBin in (SideloadHosts) and LoadedDll in (SideloadDlls)
| where HostDir == DllDir
| where not(HostDir startswith @"c:\program files\")
     and not(HostDir startswith @"c:\program files (x86)\")
     and not(HostDir startswith @"c:\windows\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, HostBin, Image, LoadedDll, ImageLoaded
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT10 (Stone Panda) Signed-Binary DLL Side-Loading Pair
id: 9e3f4a18-1c4f-4f2c-8c1c-0b3a4d27a01b
status: experimental
description: Detects APT10 / menuPass DLL side-loading where a known signed third-party host binary loads the matching malicious DLL pair from a non-install path. Pairings are taken from PwC, FireEye, JPCERT and Trend Micro reporting on RedLeaves, ANEL, UPPERCUT, ChChes and PlugX.
references:
  - https://www.pwc.co.uk/issues/cyber-security-services/insights/operation-cloud-hopper.html
  - https://www.jpcert.or.jp/english/at/2017/at170024.html
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_pair:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\jjs.exe'
      - '\GUP.exe'
      - '\mcoemcpy.exe'
      - '\EhStorAuthn.exe'
      - '\rasphone.exe'
      - '\chrome_frame_helper.exe'
      - '\vmtoolsd.exe'
      - '\mscoree.exe'
      - '\sllauncher.exe'
    ImageLoaded|endswith:
      - '\jli.dll'
      - '\libcurl.dll'
      - '\McUtil.dll'
      - '\vmtools.dll'
      - '\EhStorShell.dll'
      - '\chrome_frame_helper.dll'
      - '\version.dll'
  filter_legit_install:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Program Files\'
      - '\Program Files (x86)\'
      - '\Windows\System32\'
      - '\Windows\SysWOW64\'
  condition: selection_pair and not filter_legit_install
falsepositives:
  - Vendor utilities installed to a non-default folder (rare in managed estates)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0045
ucs.rationale: Generic 'unsigned DLL load by lsass' or 'rundll32 from Temp' rules will miss APT10 — they specifically pair a signed Oracle/McAfee/VMware/Trend host binary with a sibling DLL that hijacks an exported function. The (host-binary, DLL-name, adjacent-folder) tuple is what makes this APT10-specific rather than a generic side-loading template; the same pairings have been re-used across the Cloudhopper,

ucs.title: [LLM] APT10 Cloudhopper post-compromise: AD recon (csvde/net/dsquery) chained to remote scheduled-task creation on managed hosts
ucs.description: Hunts the APT10 Cloudhopper hand-off pattern: an MSP/jump-host operator account runs csvde / net group / dsquery / nltest to enumerate the customer domain, then within ~30 minutes uses schtasks /create /s <remoteHost> (or wmic /node:) to drop a scheduled task on a downstream client host — the documented APT10 mechanism for pivoting from MSP infrastructure to managed-customer endpoints. Tying the recon and the remote-schtasks call together on the same actor and host eliminates the high-volume noise of either signal alone.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as reconTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="csvde.exe" OR Processes.process_name="dsquery.exe" OR Processes.process_name="nltest.exe" OR (Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe") AND (Processes.process="*group \"domain admins\"*" OR Processes.process="*group \"enterprise admins\"*" OR Processes.process="*/domain*"))) AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| rename process as recon_cmd, process_name as recon_bin
| join type=inner host user [
    | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as schtasksTime values(Processes.process) as schtasks_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" AND Processes.process="*/create*" AND Processes.process="*/s *" by host Processes.user
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
  ]
| eval delta=abs(schtasksTime-reconTime)
| where delta<=1800
| eval remote_host=replace(schtasks_cmd,"(?i).*?/s\s+\\\\?\\\\?([\w\.-]+).*","\1")
| where remote_host!=host
| convert ctime(reconTime) ctime(schtasksTime)
| table reconTime schtasksTime host user recon_bin recon_cmd schtasks_cmd remote_host delta
ucs.kql:
// APT10 Cloudhopper recon-then-remote-schtasks chain on the same actor / pivot host
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let WindowMin    = 30;
let Recon =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (FileName =~ "csvde.exe")
         or (FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe")
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("group \"domain admins\"","group \"enterprise admins\"","/domain"))
         or (FileName =~ "dsquery.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("user","computer","group"))
         or (FileName =~ "nltest.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/dclist")
         or (FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe")
    | project ReconTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ReconBin = FileName, ReconCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/create" and ProcessCommandLine has "/s "
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend RemoteHost = tolower(extract(@"(?i)/s\s+\\\\?([\w\.-]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine))
| where isnotempty(RemoteHost) and RemoteHost != tolower(DeviceName)
| join kind=inner Recon on DeviceName, AccountName
| where ReconTime between (Timestamp - WindowMin * 1m .. Timestamp + WindowMin * 1m)
| project SchtasksTime = Timestamp, ReconTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, ReconTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession,
          ReconBin, ReconCmd,
          SchtasksCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          RemoteHost,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by SchtasksTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT10 Cloudhopper recon → remote schtasks chain via ASIM ImProcessCreate
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let WindowMin    = 30;
let Recon =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where (TargetProcessName has "csvde.exe")
         or (TargetProcessName has_any ("net.exe","net1.exe")
             and TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("group \"domain admins\"","group \"enterprise admins\"","/domain"))
         or (TargetProcessName has "dsquery.exe" and TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("user","computer","group"))
         or (TargetProcessName has "nltest.exe" and TargetProcessCommandLine has "/dclist")
         or (TargetProcessName has "ntdsutil.exe")
    | project ReconTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              ReconBin = TargetProcessName, ReconCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine;
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where TargetProcessName has "schtasks.exe"
| where TargetProcessCommandLine has "/create" and TargetProcessCommandLine has "/s "
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| extend RemoteHost = tolower(extract(@"(?i)/s\s+\\\\?([\w\.-]+)", 1, TargetProcessCommandLine))
| where isnotempty(RemoteHost) and RemoteHost != tolower(DvcHostname)
| join kind=inner Recon on DvcHostname, ActorUsername
| where ReconTime between (TimeGenerated - WindowMin * 1m .. TimeGenerated + WindowMin * 1m)
| project SchtasksTime = TimeGenerated, ReconTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, ReconTime),
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ReconBin, ReconCmd,
          SchtasksCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine, RemoteHost,
          Parent = ParentProcessName, ParentCmd = ParentProcessCommandLine
| order by SchtasksTime desc
ucs.rationale: The Cloudhopper / Operation TradeSecret reporting (PwC, NCSC-UK, US-CERT TA17-117A) repeatedly attribute the same hand-off shape to APT10: csvde-style domain enumeration from an MSP jump host, immediately followed by remote scheduled-task creation against managed-customer endpoints over RPC/SMB. Either signal alone is dashboard-tier — IT runs schtasks /s and admins occasionally csvde — but the sam

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?
